sticker.alt: Full sticker design: a long horizontal illustration featuring 22 coral reef creatures and features
sticker._note: referenceWidth/referenceHeight MUST match the actual pixel dimensions of full-sticker.png exactly, or every creature's position will be misaligned. Update these the moment you know the real sticker size.

creatures.name: Forereef coral community
creatures.alt: PLACEHOLDER ALT TEXT for creature 01 — replace with a short visual description
creatures.description: The outer reef is called the fore reef and serves as the primary physical barrier between the outer ocean and lagoon. The forereef on Moorea has experienced many disturbances including bleaching and cyclones. Cyclones can sometimes act as a slate-clearing event, making space for new corals to settle and grow. After the most recent bleaching of 2019, the majority of corals that recovered were from the same genera, Pocillopora, shown here. Another bleaching event more recently resulted in the widespread deaths of these corals, which are now sitting on the reef, hollowing out from within.
papers: Burgess, S. C. et al. (2021). Response diversity in corals: Hidden differences in bleaching mortality among cryptic Pocillopora species. Ecology, 102(6), e03324.
Scafidi, K. C. et al. (2026). Remnant hollowed out dead coral skeleton branches defer coral community recovery. Plos one, 21(3), e0339527.

creatures.name: Fringing reef coral community
creatures.alt: A cluster of light brown corals with sea urchins around them
creatures.description: The reef closest to the island is called the fringing reef. This ecosystem is first to absorb any pollutants that run off the land into the water and, in Moorea, is characterized by many corals from the genus Porites. Here you ca see the Porites rus corals creating a habitat for sea urchins, which can be found in high abundance in these nearshore environments.
creatures.papers: Edmunds, P. J. et al. (2025). Long‐term community dynamics are heterogeneous between fringing‐and fore‐reef habitats on an Indo‐Pacific coral reef. Ecosphere, 16(10), e70398.

creatures.name: Poti Marara
creatures.alt: A red fishing boat, moving quickly over the water.
creatures.description: "Poti" means boat in Tahitian, and "marara" means flying fish, illustrating the speed of these vessels as their V shaped hull cuts seamlessly into the water. With a bow-positioned tiller, the driver is able to steer the boat with one hand, and spear fish from their high vantage point with the other. The front tiller steering allows for tight turns and easy mobility.

creatures.name: Magnificent sea anemone
creatures.alt: PLACEHOLDER ALT TEXT — replace with a short visual description
creatures.description: The magnificent sea anemone is a charismatic creature on the reef, easily recognizable thanks to the mutually beneficial relationship between these anemone and the fishes that live on and around them. Since sea anemones are in the Phylum Cnidaria (the same one as sea jellies), they have similar stinging cells, called cnidocytes, located in their tentacles. So be careful not to touch these mesmerizing creatures, in case your skin reacts to the sting.

creatures.name: Humpback whale
creatures.description: Whale season is from roughly the middle of June until xxx in Moorea. During this time, you can hear the whales communicating underwater while diving and may be lucky enough to see them beaching offshore throughout the day. Occasionally, the mothers will bring their babies near the reef, protecting them from predators by using the structural boundary of the reef as a natural barrier on one side.
